name: HMS Challenger Expedition
description: The first true oceanographic expedition, sailing 68,890 nautical miles and establishing the foundations of modern marine biology. The resulting 50-volume report took 23 years to publish.

name: Valdivia Deep-Sea Expedition
description: The first German deep-sea expedition, deploying early electric lights to observe creatures in situ. Chun's illustrated reports set a new standard for scientific illustration of deep-sea life.

name: Albatross Pacific Expedition
description: A series of Pacific surveys aboard the purpose-built research steamer Albatross, the first vessel designed specifically for oceanographic research. Agassiz dredged thousands of new specimens from the Pacific deep.

name: Scripps Institution Midwater Survey
description: A systematic survey of midwater fauna off the California coast, pioneering the use of fine-mesh trawls to capture fragile gelatinous organisms that previous expeditions had destroyed.

name: Johnson Sea-Link ROV Program
description: One of the first sustained programs to use remotely operated vehicles for deep-sea biological surveys, capturing live footage of creatures previously known only from damaged trawl specimens.

name: MBARI Deep-Sea Census
description: A systematic ROV-based census of deep-sea life in Monterey Canyon, producing the most comprehensive video catalogue of mid-water and abyssal fauna in the northeastern Pacific.
